Overall Proportional Match
Body Fat % estimates the proportion of your body that is fat using waist and neck measurements. FFMI (Fat-Free Mass Index) measures your muscle mass relative to height — similar to BMI but only counting lean tissue. An FFMI of 25 is widely considered the approximate ceiling for drug-free athletes.
Measurement Breakdown
Best match → Top  ·  Most work needed → Bottom  ·  | = target
What to Work On
Closest to target → Top  ·  Biggest gaps → Bottom

Nutrition Plan
Personalized macros · 7-day meal plan

Enter your body weight and your estimated daily calorie burn (TDEE — Total Daily Energy Expenditure). Based on your goal, Physique Lab will build a plan that hits 1 gram of protein per pound of bodyweight to protect and build muscle, with 25% of calories from healthy fats and the rest from carbohydrates for energy.

If you're looking to lean out, the plan applies a 300-calorie daily deficit below your TDEE — enough to lose roughly 0.5–0.75 lbs per week without sacrificing muscle. If you're building, it adds a 300-calorie surplus to fuel growth. Your waist measurement from the analysis is used to give you a personalized recommendation on which goal makes the most sense for where you are right now.

What actually makes up your TDEE: roughly 60–70% is your Basal Metabolic Rate (BMR — the energy your body burns just staying alive at rest), with the remainder split between digesting food, everyday movement outside the gym (walking, chores, fidgeting), and the exercise you do. Because that mix is different for everyone, the most accurate way to find your real number is to wear a fitness tracker (Whoop, Apple Watch, Garmin, Fitbit, Oura, or similar) for 3–5 normal days of eating and activity, then use the "calories burned" or "total energy" figure it reports for an average day as your TDEE here. If you don't have one, use the estimator below to get a rough starting number instead — then adjust it up or down by 100–150 calories every couple of weeks based on how your actual weight trend compares to the plan's prediction.

Once generated, you'll get a full 7-day meal plan with two daily options (A & B) so you can rotate based on what you have on hand. Each day includes breakfast, lunch, dinner, and a snack — all built around whole foods: lean proteins, complex carbs, and healthy fats.

This gives a rough starting estimate using the Mifflin-St Jeor formula (age, sex, height, weight, and activity level). It's a reasonable starting point if you don't have a tracker — but a wearable worn for a few days will always be more accurate to your actual metabolism and lifestyle.
